title: L3Harris Backlog Inflection — Record $40.7B Backlog + 1.4x Book-to-Bill + $25B MAC Pending
summary: L3Harris Q1 2026 reported a record $40.7B backlog (~2x annual revenue) and 1.4x consolidated book-to-bill (2.2x international) — with an additional $25B of pending MAC munitions orders not yet in backlog. The market is pricing LHX on legacy comm/EW multiples while it transitions into a missile-electronics / strike-systems revenue mix.
catalyst: On April 30, 2026, L3Harris reported Q1 2026 orders of $7.8B against $5.7B of revenue — a 1.4x book-to-bill ratio that drove backlog to a record $40.7B, up from ~$24B two years prior. Management separately disclosed approximately $25B in pending Munitions Acceleration Council (MAC) orders under negotiation that are not yet in backlog — these would push pro-forma backlog above $65B (~3x revenue). Q1 revenue grew 15% organically, GAAP EPS rose 33%, and segment operating margin reached 15.7%. The Q2 2026 earnings print (~late July) and the MAC contract finalizations are the next two falsifiable checkpoints.
thesis: LHX is in the middle of a backlog-driven re-rating that the market hasn't fully recognized. The 1.4x book-to-bill (above the >1.2 green-signal threshold from our backlog framework) and international 2.2x book-to-bill reflect both the Western re-armament cycle and LHX's missile/EW content rising in a Hormuz-era spending mix. With backlog approaching 2x revenue today and ~3x pro-forma once MAC orders close, revenue visibility for FY27-28 is unusually high. The stock trades at ~23x forward earnings, in line with the broader defense complex, despite a faster organic growth rate (15% Q1 vs ~7-9% for the primes) and accelerating order momentum. The pre-mortem case: a defense-budget unwind in 2027 caps the multiple before the backlog converts.
counter_thesis: Defense procurement budgets are at cycle highs in real terms — a fiscal retrenchment in 2027 (post-election, debt-ceiling driven, or a Ukraine/Hormuz resolution) could cap order momentum even if backlog converts to revenue. Funded vs unfunded backlog mix matters: if a meaningful portion of the $40.7B is IDIQ ceilings rather than firm-funded commitments, the visibility is overstated. The $25B MAC pending is precisely the kind of disclosure that can slip — contract negotiations dragging from Q3 to Q1 2027 would pressure the multiple. LHX margins also remain below LMT/NOC peers; if execution doesn't deliver promised margin expansion the re-rating thesis weakens. Finally, the rest of the defense complex (LMT, NOC, RTX, GD) already prints 1.1-1.2x book-to-bill — LHX's 1.4x is good but not a unique signal at the sector level.

ticker_rationale.LHX: L3Harris is the largest defense electronics pure-play — communications, EW, missile electronics, space systems. The 1.4x book-to-bill is above the defense prime average and is driven by both US replenishment (Aerojet Rocketdyne integration delivering solid rocket motors) and international demand (2.2x international book-to-bill). With $40.7B backlog and $25B pending MAC, FY27 revenue is highly visible. Trading at $317 / 23.5x forward earnings vs $360-$390 sell-side median targets (Buy consensus from 16 of 21 analysts), risk/reward is asymmetric on a 12-month basis.

trade_construction.target_12mo: LHX $385 (+21%) — anchored to sell-side median target and 27x forward earnings on FY27E EPS
trade_construction.stop_loss: LHX $270 (-15%) — would imply MAC orders not closing and book-to-bill reverting below 1.1x

catalyst_calendar.event: Q2 2026 earnings — watch consolidated book-to-bill, international book-to-bill, MAC contract status
Q3 2026 earnings — first read on MAC contract conversions to firm backlog
FY27 US defense appropriations final — munitions account funding level

risks: Funded vs unfunded backlog mix not fully disclosed — some portion of $40.7B may be IDIQ ceilings rather than firm orders, overstating visibility
MAC pending $25B could slip 6-12 months in negotiation, pressuring 2027 organic growth
Defense budget retrenchment in FY28 if Hormuz / Ukraine resolves or post-election fiscal reset hits
Margin expansion thesis (LHX trailing LMT/NOC) requires successful Aerojet integration — execution risk
IOWN score is 71 (HOLD) — quality is decent but the trade is primarily a re-rating call, not a compounder thesis

invalidation.trigger: LHX consolidated book-to-bill prints below 1.0x for 2 consecutive quarters
MAC pending orders formally cancelled or reduced by >50%
US defense outlays cut >10% YoY in FY28 budget
International book-to-bill drops below 1.2x (NATO re-armament rolls over)
